pattern: A search over subsets of the target's remaining letters, applying one sticker at a time, finds the fewest stickers, with letter-count memoization pruning the state space.
hints: Represent the part of the target still unspelled as a state; each sticker consumes some of those letters.
From a state, try every sticker, subtract the letters it provides, and recurse on what remains.

Use a bitmask of which target positions are still needed, or memoize on the remaining letter-count tuple. dp[state] is the minimum stickers; for each sticker greedily fill needed letters and take 1 + dp[next]. Unreachable states are infinity, reported as -1 at the end.

edge_inputs.description: three stickers combine to form the target
impossible because a needed letter is absent
one sticker reused to cover repeated letters
missing a single letter makes it impossible
redundant sticker should not be chosen

stress.description: small random sticker set guaranteed to cover a short random target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    alpha = 'abcde'
    tlen = rng.randrange(1, 9)
    target = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(tlen))
    ns = rng.randrange(2, 6)
    stickers = []
    for _ in range(ns):
        sl = rng.randrange(1, 6)
        stickers.append(''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(sl)))
    # 60% of the time guarantee solvability by adding stickers covering each letter
    if rng.random() < 0.6:
        for ch in set(target):
            stickers.append(ch)
    return [stickers, target]
stress.note: short targets and small alphabet keep the exponential DP fast; mix of solvable and impossible
